findings.description: flush() opens and tears down a brand-new SMTP connection inside the per-message loop, paying the full TCP+EHLO+STARTTLS+AUTH handshake cost N times; open one connection before the loop and reuse server.sendmail() for all messages, quitting once after.

_seen grows unbounded across flushes (only cleared via explicit clear_dedup_cache), a slow memory leak in a long-lived queue; dedup semantics keyed only on msg.to is a correctness concern deferred to critic-logic.
